webLinks.linkText: White Bay Power Station - Biennale of Sydney

properties.publicTransport: Route 441 and 442 buses run regularly to Rozelle. As well, route 443 heritage buses will run on weekends and public holidays. See details at transportnsw.info
properties.otherAdvice:
The Biennale of Sydney will be held at White Bay Power Station open from Tuesday-Sundays 10am-5pm daily, from Saturday 14 March to Sunday 14 June. The exhibition will also be open on public holidays.

Traffic will be heavy on roads approaching White Bay Power Station as crowds travel to and from the event,

Attendees driving should carpool with their group, plan to arrive early as local parking is limited.

Motorists should allow extra travel time on Victoria Rd, the City West Link and Robert St.

roads.mainStreet: Robert Street

properties.otherAdvice:
The Enchanted Forest is returning to Shellharbour from Friday 24 April until Sunday 17 May.

For the duration of the event, a speed reduction will be in place on Shellharbour Rd.

roads.mainStreet: Shellharbour Road

properties.publicTransport: Major trackwork is taking place on metro, trains and light rail network. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Bikes & Bulls at Qudos Bank Arena in Sydney Olympic Park on Saturday 16 May. Show starts at 6.30pm

Traffic delays are expected as attendees make their way to Olympic Park each day. Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

Fans driving should pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space, available until 11:59pm the night before the show unless sold out prior. If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Road closures may be in place. Check out the Sydney Olympic Park Authority interactive map for more details.

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road

properties.publicTransport: Buses replace trains on the Central Coast & Newcastle Line as well as the Hunter Line due to planned weekend trackwork. Free event shuttle bus services will operate for the game. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
A-League Men Semi Final Newcastle Jets v Syd FC at McDonald Jones Stadium. Kick-off at 7.40pm. Full time at approx. 9.40pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on Turton Rd, Griffiths Rd, Newcastle Rd, Lambton Rd and Bridges Rd through Broadmeadow while fans are making their way to and from the stadium.

For passenger drop-off, use the allocated 10-minute loading zone in the McDonald Jones Stadium car park. Entry is via the driveway off Turton Road.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.﻿

roads.mainStreet: Griffiths Road

properties.publicTransport: ﻿Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Sydney 10 running event at Sydney Olympic Park on Sunday May 17. 10km events start from 6:45am, 5km starts from 9:10am and 2.5km starts from 9:55am.

Expect traffic delays approaching Sydney Olympic Park and entering car parks as the precinct will be busy.

Motorists should allow extra travel time on the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

Carpool with your group pre-book your parking online ahead of time to secure a space – available until 11:59pm the night before the event unless sold out prior. If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Sydney Olympic Park Authority will close local roads in the precinct to ensure pedestrian safety during events. View the interactive map for closure details

arrangementElements.html:
From 1.30am to 1.30pm 

  • Dawn Fraser Avenue from Olympic Boulevard to Edwin Flack Avenue 
  • Marjorie Jackson Parkway from Australia Avenue to Bennelong Parkway 
  • Holker Busway from Hill Road to Kevin Coombs Avenue
arrangementElements.title: Road Closures

roads.mainStreet: Dawn Fraser Avenue

properties.otherAdvice:
PBC Baby Expo at Sydney Showground on Saturday 16 & Sunday 17 May. Expo starts at 10am and finishes at 4pm daily.

Attendees are encouraged to plan their trip and consider all the available travel options ahead of time.  

Motorists should allow plenty of extra travel time on roads approaching Olympic Park including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

Sydney Olympic Park Authority may close local roads in the precinct and traffic congestion is expected before and after the events. For road closure details, visit the Sydney Olympic Park Authority interactive map.

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road, Centenary Drive

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on public transport. Express trains and Major Event Buses will run. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
State of Origin Game I at Accor Stadium on Wednesday 27 May. Gates open at 5:00pm. Peter Mulholland Cup kick-off 6pm. Pre-game entertainment starts 7:30pm. State of Origin Game I kick-off 8:05pm. Full-time 10pm.

With around 80,000 people expected to attend State of Origin Game I, significant delays are expected on roads approaching the precinct.  

Motorists should allow plenty of extra travel time on roads approaching Olympic Park including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

  • PARKING: Do not drive unless you have pre-booked parking. Pre-book online ahead of time to secure a space. A flat rate $35 parking fee applies. 
  • If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.
  • Carpool with your group and allow plenty of extra travel time as traffic will be heavy during the busy weeknight peak.
  • Alternatively, consider parking at a nearby train station commuter car park and continuing your trip by train to Olympic Park, at no extra cost.

Sydney Olympic Park Authority will close local roads in the precinct and traffic congestion is expected before and after the match. For road closure details, visit the Sydney Olympic Park Authority interactive map.

arrangementElements.html:
Sydney Olympic Park Authority will close local roads in the precinct to ensure pedestrian safety during the event and to accommodate additional transport services. For road closure details, view the interactive map.

properties.publicTransport: Major trackwork is taking place on Metro and L2/L3 light rail services. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Thousands are expected to attend the Flower Market by CJ Hendry from Friday 15 to Sunday 17 May in The Domain Sydney

Motorists should allow extra travel time, be aware of increased pedestrian activity and use alternative routes while closures are in place.

If you are catching rideshare or taxis, use the dedicated drop-off location on Hospital Road at Art Gallery Road. For pick-ups after the event head towards Elizabeth Street or Sir John Young Crescent.

roads.mainStreet: Art Gallery Rd, Mrs Macquaries Rd & Hospital Rd

properties.otherAdvice:
Special event clearways will be in place at the following locations to facilitate Sydney Trains trackwork replacement bus operations:

  • Chalmers Street, Surry Hills. western side of Chalmers Street between Cleveland Street and Devonshire Street and eastern side between Cleveland Street and Rutland Street.

Special event clearways are strict no-parking zones and also apply to local residents and Mobility Scheme Parking Permit holders. Vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies.

roads.mainStreet: Chalmers Street

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
May shows at the TikTok Entertainment Centre:

  • Saturday 16 May: FIVE at 8pm
  • Monday 18 & Tuesday 19 May: Split Enz - Forever Enz Tour 2026. Shows start nightly at 8pm
  • Wednesday 20 & Thursday 21 May: Michelle Obama. Shows starts nightly at 7.30pm
  • Saturday 30 May: Nancy Ajram at 7.30pm

Traffic may be heavier than usual as attendees travel to and from the event. Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

From Friday 22 May, traffic delays are expected as Vivid takes place in and around the Harbour Foreshore. Ticketholders to ICC events are urged to plan ahead if attending a show while Vivid is also on.

roads.mainStreet: Darling Drive

properties.publicTransport: Travel on transport is included in match tickets and additional light rail services will run. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Super Rugby: Waratahs v Brumbies at Allianz Stadium on Friday 22 May. Kick-off at 7.35pm, full time around 9.20pm.

Traffic delays are expected on roads approaching Moore Park as rugby fans make their way to the precinct in the already busy evening peak.

Motorists should carpool and allow plenty of extra travel time on roads including the Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St, Anzac Pde and Lang Rd.

Changed traffic conditions:

  • Driver Avenue will be partially closed outside the stadium from 4.30pm and will reopen once crowds have safely cleared the area.   
  • No through-traffic will be available on Driver Avenue, however access to EP3 grass car park and drop-off areas maintained from Lang Road. Car Parks open from 4.35pm. 
  • Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 3pm to 11pm on sections of Albion Street and Lang Road, and from 6am to 11pm on sections of Cleveland Street and Fitzroy Street. 

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

From 3pm to 11pm

  • Albion Street between Bourke Street and Flinders Street (both sides) 
  • Lang Road between Anzac Parade and Errol Flynn Boulevard (northern side)

properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Bulldogs v Storm at Accor Stadium. NRL kick-off at 8pm. Full time around 9.45pm.

Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

If you need to drive, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space. Follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Sydney Olympic Park Authority may close some local roads to accommodate increased public transport services and ensure pedestrian safety during events. For road closure details, visit the Sydney Olympic Park interactive map for more details.

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road, Centenary Drive

properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Dragons v Warriors at St George Venues Jubilee Stadium. NSW Cup kick off at 3.10, NRL Kick-off at 5.30pm, full time at 7.15pm

Traffic will be heavier than usual in both directions on the Princes Hwy approaching Kogarah as fans travel to the stadium.

Local roads around the stadium may close ahead of match start including Jubilee Ave, Park St and English St. Roads will re-open once crowds have cleared the area.

Fans driving to the game should allow extra time for parking and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

roads.mainStreet: Jubilee Avenue, English Street, Park Street

properties.publicTransport: Additional route 199 buses will also run towards Manly and Mona Vale after the game to help you get home. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Sea Eagles v Titans at 4 Pines Park in Brookvale. NRL kick-off at 7.35pm and full time around 9.20pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on roads approaching the stadium, including Pittwater Rd, Old Pittwater Rd, Warringah Rd and Beacon Hill Rd.

Motorists should plan to arrive early as parking will be in high demand and also be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

NOTE: There is no parking on the southern side of Federal Pde between Pine Ave and Alfred Rd permitted from 8pm the night before the match until the crowd has safely cleared the stadium after full time on game day.

roads.mainStreet: Pittwater Road

properties.otherAdvice:
Cancer Foundation Race Day at Royal Randwick Racecourse. First race from 11am, last race from 4.25pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual on Anzac Pde, Doncaster Ave, High St, Alison Rd and Darley Rd as crowds travel to and from the event.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

roads.mainStreet: Anzac Parade, Alison Road, High Street

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on all modes of public transport for both games. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip
properties.otherAdvice:
AFL: GWS Giants v Brisbane at ENGIE Stadium. First Bounce at 12.30pm and full time at 3pm.

Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

If you need to drive, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space. Follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Roads within the precinct will be closed between 1.30am and 1pm for the Dementia Australia Memory Walk & Jog also taking place in the precinct. For road closure details, visit the Sydney Olympic Park interactive map for more details.

College St (including cycleway) will be closed between William St and Prince Albert Rd from 7:15pm to 8:15pm for a processional march.

Participants will assemble at St Mary’s Cathedral from 6pm. The processional march will start at 7:30pm and finish by 8pm.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and also be aware of increased pedestrian activity.

Expect traffic delays approaching Sydney Olympic Park and entering car parks as the precinct will be busy.

Motorists should allow extra travel time on the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

Carpool with your group pre-book your parking online ahead of time to secure a space – available until 11:59pm the night before the event unless sold out prior. If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Sydney Olympic Park Authority will close local roads in the precinct to ensure pedestrian safety during events. View the interactive map for closure details

properties.publicTransport: Sydney Metro services will not operate due to planned maintenance, catch trains to North Sydney. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Beer, Footy & Food Festival at North Sydney Oval on Saturday 16 & Sunday 17 May. Festival starts daily at 12pm, Saturday finish at 5.45pm & Sunday finish at 4.45pm

Traffic may be heavier than usual on roads approaching and around North Sydney Oval throughout the weekend.

Allow extra time and travel with a group if you have to drive as parking is limited.

Limited street parking is available in the area and Ridge St car park is within walking distance of the Oval. Fees apply.

roads.mainStreet: Miller Street

properties.publicTransport:
Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip on public transport.

Trains run regularly to Albion Park station on the South Coast Line and is less than 100 metres from the airshow entry gate. Local buses also service the area, with bus stops within walking distance. 

A designated ‘Park and Ride’ parking area will be available at the corner of Croome Road and Tongarra Road with shuttle buses to get you to and from the event. This offsite parking area will have a free shuttle bus service to and from the main airshow gates from 8:30am until 6:00pm.
properties.otherAdvice:
Thousands are expected to attend the Wings Over Shellharbour Airshow at Shellharbour Airport. Gates open daily at 9am, with the show starting from 9am and finishing at 5pm.

Traffic will be heavy along the Princes Hwy, the M1 Princes Mwy and Tongarra Road with changed traffic conditions in place approaching Park and Ride facilities at the corner of Tongarra Rd and Croome Rd. Motorists are advised to take care and allow extra travel time in the area over the weekend.

Local road closures and changed traffic conditions will be in place around the airport. Follow the directions of traffic controllers on site.

Those attending the event are advised to catch public transport and are reminded that no general parking is available at Shellharbour Airport.

roads.mainStreet: Princes Highway, M1 Princes Motorway, Tongarra Road

webLinks.linkText: TfNSW Vivid Sydney - top tips to plan your trip

properties.publicTransport: Trains will not drop off at Circular Quay from 6pm-11pm on Saturdays + Sun 7 Jun. Buses will relocate to Martin Place from 6pm Fridays and 5pm on weekends. Light rail will not run between Circular Quay and Town Hall from 6pm Fridays and 5pm on weekends. Ferries will be busy on weekends and access to wharves at Circular Quay will be different on busy nights – follow signage. For more details and top tips to plan your trip, visit transportnsw.info.
properties.otherAdvice:
Vivid Sydney returns to light up the city for its 16th year across 23 consecutive nights from Friday 22 May to Saturday 13 June.

The city is no place for cars with road closures and clearway parking restrictions in place each night in Circular Quay, The Rocks, Dawes Point and Millers Point between 3pm and midnight daily, and until 1am on Saturday nights.

Drone Shows are back on this year but will take place at Cockle Bay in Darling Harbour twice a night on Sunday to Wednesdays only (excl Sun 7 June).

Road closures:

  • Monday to Thursdays: from 3pm local roads in The Rocks, Millers Point and Dawes Point will close including sections of Lower Fort St, Merriman St and Rodens Ln. From 5pm additional roads will close including sections of George St, Argyle St and Argyle Pl, Harrington St and Pottinger St. On Mon-Wed drone show nights only, closures will also be in place from 6.30pm-10.30pm on Wheat Rd at King St Wharf/Cockle Bay,
  • Fridays: additional closures will be in place from 3pm on High St and High Ln. From 5pm additional closures will be in place including all of Argyle St, and sections of Hickson Rd, George St, Essex St, Upper Fort St and Watson Rd. From 6pm roads in Circular Quay including Pitt, Loftus, Young, Albert and Phillip Streets will close.
  • Saturdays + Sun 7 Jun: local closures will be in place from 3pm, and major roads in Circular Quay, The Rocks, Millers Point and Dawes Point will close from 5pm. Additionally Grosvenor St/Bridge St between York St and Loftus St will be closed - no east/west access via Bridge St during this time. Roads remain closed until 1am. Wheat Rd will close from 7pm to 11pm.
  • Sundays + Mon 8 Jun: local closures will be in place from 3pm, and key roads in Circular Quay, The Rocks, Millers Point and Dawes Point will close from 5pm to midnight listed above. On Sunday drone show nights only, closures will also be in place from 6.30pm-10.30pm on Wheat Rd at King St Wharf/Cockle Bay,
  • 12pm Friday to 7am Monday each weekend, George St will close between Lower Fort St and Hickson Rd for The Rocks Markets. 

Special event clearways will be in effect on most major roads in the city between 3pm and midnight daily during Vivid Sydney. Some clearways will be in effect earlier from 1pm and 2pm on local roads.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

roads.mainStreet: George St, Hickson Rd, Bridge St
roads.suburb: Circular Quay, The Rocks, Barangaroo, Darling Harbour

properties.otherAdvice:
Changed traffic conditions will be in place on Les Darcy Drive (New England Highway) as well as some local road closures.

Traffic controllers will be on site to direct motorists.

Special event clearways will be in place continuously from 2am Sat 16 May to 2am Mon 18 May at the following location to facilitate rail replacement bus operations for Metro North West trackwork:

  • Beecroft Rd, southbound between Carlingford Rd and Bridge Street.


Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and businesses with parking permits or TfNSW Mobility Scheme permit holders. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

properties.otherAdvice:
AFL: Swans v Collingwood game at the SCG. First bounce at 7.30pm, full time at 10.10pm

With up to 40,000 fans attending significant traffic delays are expected on roads approaching Moore Park during the already busy evening peak.

Motorists should allow plenty of extra travel time on roads including the Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St, Anzac Pde and Lang Rd.

Attendees are urged to catch public transport and leave the car at home to avoid significant traffic delays and the high demand for parking.

Changed traffic conditions:

  • Driver Avenue will be partially closed outside the stadium from 3pm and will reopen once crowds have safely cleared the area.  
  • No through-traffic will be available on Driver Avenue, however access to EP2 Kippax car park will be maintained via Moore Park Road and access to EP3 grass car park and drop-off areas will be maintained from Lang Road. Grass car parks open from 4.30pm. 
  • Special event clearways will be in effect on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 3pm to midnight on sections of Albion Street, Moore Park Road and Lang Road, and from 6am to midnight on sections of Cleveland Street and Fitzroy Street 

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.
